consensus_score.technique_summary: V18 (SURG25 desk-mount) is a technically complete but inefficient run at 162.95s. The defining feature is a unique bottleneck inversion: cutting phase is the FASTEST in the entire V-series at 6.7s (previous best ~26s), while throws 2 and 3 combined consume ~97s (60% of total task time) due to prolonged regrasping and tail management. The knot sequence is correct, hand switching is confirmed right-left-right, the drain is closed with no gap, and no avulsion occurred. This represents a cutting-skill breakthrough paired with a throw-efficiency regression at an unfamiliar station.
improvement_suggestions: Throws 2+3 at 97s combined = 60% of total time - PRIMARY bottleneck, not cutting
Needle load at 14.9s is 3x slower than peak (5s) - station unfamiliarity
Practice throw advancement with consistent tail tension to reduce regrasping pauses
Leverage the new 6.7s cutting skill - the end-game efficiency is already mastered
Station SURG25 is a new setup - baseline recalibration needed before throw regression can be isolated from equipment effects
strengths: FASTEST CUT PHASE in the entire V-series at 6.7s (previous best ~26s) - breakthrough
No visible gap - complete drain closure
Correct knot sequence with confirmed hand switching
Drain not avulsed - safe tissue handling
